reviews.review_title: Could be a tiny bit better
reviews.review_description: I picked the standing mode thinking that it would scale things to my size...it did not, I'm four foot nine. I sadly could not see through the windows, BUT its a good game with good mechanics and guns, I'm buying the real game.🙂

So, when I put my game in stand up mode, i was too tall to even go through doors, I had to crouch irl just to go into the doorway. I couldn't really even do anything. Please tell me how to change my mode to sit mode so im shorter.

developer_response.body:
Hi there.

Thanks for checking out the Shotwood Demo!

The in-game "height adjust" option can be found in the "comfort" section in the Options Tablet - this device can be found on the bed in the home environment.

When you select "standing" or "sat" while creating a new game (the first time you start the demo) it will automatically set up some comfort and inventory options for you based on your choice. These are saved to your profile, and loaded when you return to the game later. The "height adjust" option is one of these - it boosts you to a standing height from your seated height.

The game does try to disable the height adjustment when it detects you're returning while stood up, though it's not able to guess correctly for everyone, and it doesn't adjust itself mid-game (so if you stand up mid-session you'll probably be way too tall).

Anyway, grab the options tablet from the bed in the home area, tap "comfort" and you'll see the "height adjust" option in there. Set it to "none" and you should be good to go!

Oh, also, if you're still at the wrong height after setting "height adjust" to "none", be sure to check that your floor height is set correctly in your headset/Quest settings (outside of the game). This should be aligned with your real floor (outside of VR).

I hope this helps. Have fun in there!

reviews.review_description: A shoot house is a natural for VR and not enough games take advantage of that.  Two early gripes: 1) Hard to tell civilian looking opponents from hostages. 2) Hate having opponents spawn into an area already cleared (like a room with no other door).

The shooter was fun to play the game setup kinda didn’t make sense because I couldn’t find the level selection for like 5 minutes but I was able to explore the map and find it after a few minutes. The game is fun and I can’t wait for the full game to launch, even though I might not be able to buy it. I bet it be fun for everyone else who buys it

I’m gonna be complete honest, I loved this game. It was very comfortable to play and very realistic. I’m honestly very hyped to play the full game, so I can get access to more weapons, because I want to see if there’s any difference between the demos guns and the full games guns

Overall this is a really good gun game. First of all the realistic features in this game is really good, the bullets don’t actually go straight, they mostly go side to side and isn’t accurate which is realistic. Second, in the shotgun, I like how you can load the shells and then use  the controller to close the chamber. Third, the mechanics are very smooth. When you drop the mag, load in a new one, put a single bullet into the mag, loading shells in the shotgun, all of this is super smooth. Overall I would give this game 5 stars because it so good and underrated.

Loved this game so much got it like last night and was blown away by how good it is like ive never seen a vr game as good as this the area after the grevance room was such a cool little bit to explore and was such a good way to enhance the experience overall really enjoyed the demo and will most likely get the full game one thing that could be added tho is a bow and arrow

reviews.review_title: Solid Gun Mechanics vs Cardboard CutOuts
reviews.review_description: The gun mechanics in this game is solid and the guns pack a punch. Most of the combat takes place in close quarters and involve CQC which is difficult to pull off in VR as the longer barrelled guns like rifles and shotguns will clip into the wall. Somewhat disappointing the enemies are all non-animated and immobile cardboard cut-outs of terrorists, zombies that "attack" back with occasional hostages thrown in that you are not to shoot at. Only the shotgun, handgun and grenades are available in the demo but there are many more guns in the full game. IMO, Crisis Brigade 2 Reloaded though a little more expensive and without free locomotion does it better.
Shot wood will need a story line though
I’m too poor to buy the real game but I like the concept
I have it on snap turn i want to change it to smooth turn and i don’t like teleport movement either. I just picked it for the tutorial.

developer_response.body: Hi there. Turn mode, and other options, can be changed by using the settings tablet (it's on the bed - to your right side in the home environment, when you first load in). The screen will turn on when you pick it up. I hope this helps. Thanks for checking out the Shotwood demo!

reviews.review_description: This game is incredible, the graphics and guns are:👍 the sounds and even just picking up the gun is incredible and its even funny!? This may be the next best shooting game, its like pavlov had a baby with hotdogs horseshoes and handgrenades, its so good man, and your telling me its the DEMO?!!! Simply amazing game 10/5 would buy the full game!!
I can tell that these devs have put in a lot of time and effort. Hopefully they will make more games like this one.

This is one of my favorite vr shooter of all time for a series of reasons, As a gun nerd it felt amazing to use the firearms while reloading and firing, it is really well put for a demo despite the fact that some of the content isn’t available, 10/10 would recommend.
